item.title: LOCAL GETAWAYS | A peaceful getaway
item.description: Tucked away in the lush highlands between Nadi and Lautoka, Koroyanitu Heritage Park is one of Fiji’s hidden gems for nature lovers and adventure seekers. Home to spectacular waterfalls, panoramic mountain views and rich biodiversity, the community-managed park offers visitors the chance to experience Fiji beyond its beaches. Whether you’re looking for a leisurely walk-through native forest, a cha
item.content:encoded:
Tucked away in the lush highlands between Nadi and Lautoka, Koroyanitu Heritage Park is one of Fiji’s hidden gems for nature lovers and adventure seekers.

Home to spectacular waterfalls, panoramic mountain views and rich biodiversity, the community-managed park offers visitors the chance to experience Fiji beyond its beaches. Whether you’re looking for a leisurely walk-through native forest, a challenging climb to the summit of Mt Batilamu, or an overnight stay in a traditional village lodge, Koroyanitu combines breathtaking scenery with authentic Fijian culture and warm village hospitality. Koroyanitu Heritage Park near Nadi is home to some of Fiji’s most scenic hikes. Whether you opt for a day hike or an overnight trekking experience, the stunning landscape and rich Fijian culture make it truly memorable for a local getaway adventure and also for tourists.

Here is what www,fiji,travel website has to say:

The Park features three main trails – two shorter trails each to a different waterfall and a longer, more strenuous hike to the summit of Mt. Batilamu, Fiji’s fourth highest mountain (1,100m or 3,608ft).

Koroyanitu National Heritage Park is a stunning 35km sanctuary established in 1989 to protect Fiji’s unique flora and fauna. With waterfalls and camping spots including cozy lodges and breathtaking views out to the islands, Koroyanitu’s hiking trails are a must-do for any serious hiker.

Located just 15km or a 30-minute drive inland from Lautoka City, Abaca Village is the ‘base camp’ for your adventure.

The dirt road to the village can get a little rough during the rainy season, so a 4WD truck or a arranged taxi familiar with the route is the best way to get there.

All tracks kick off at the Visitor Centre in Abaca Village.

They’ll provide you with trail maps, expert advice on the best routes, updates on current conditions and can also arrange homestays, village lunches and a personal guide if you want.

Also let them know If you’re a keen birder in need of a knowledgeable guide.

The Park is one of the best birdwatching spots in Fiji and home to rare birds like the Masked Shining Parrot, Fiji Parrotfinch, Pacific Harrier, Red Avadavat, and the Fiji White-eye.

Rather than do it all in one day, you can break up your hike and explore the Park at a more leisurely pace if you stay overnight in a village homestay or one of two rustic lodges.

Nase Lodge, a 40 minute walk from the village, is the perfect base to explore Koroyanitu’s waterfall hikes.

But for a truly unforgettable experience, camp out in the lodge at the top of Mt. Batilamu, where you’ll be treated to a breathtaking sunset and sunrise over Nadi and the nearby islands.

You can then continue your hike down to the village the next day.

Both lodges sleep multiple people and have basic cooking and toilet facilities.

As the hike starts off in Abaca Village, you should bring a sulu (sarong) to wear over your pants within the village boundaries.

A small sevusevu (kava bundle) is also respectful but not essential.

If you need, you can buy this from the Lautoka food market before you head inland.

Like any remote hike, pack plenty of water, energy-boosting snacks, and lightweight, long-sleeved gear to tackle the sun, mosquitoes, and long grass.

Don’t forget sturdy shoes, a map or GPS, and some insect repellent.

Sunscreen and a hat will keep you protected, and a rain jacket will ensure you’re prepared for any unexpected showers.

If you’re staying overnight in the lodges, make sure to cozy up in warm layers (it can get chilly) and bring basic ingredients to cook an easy dinner and breakfast, a flashlight and a deck of playing cards for entertainment.

Also bring some swimmers and a spare change of clothes if you intend to swim in any of the waterfalls.

Start early to catch the wildlife and skip the midday heat, and don’t forget plenty of water and snacks.

We also recommend taking a local guide, especially on the longer hike up Mount Batilamu for extra safety on the trail and their excellent knowledge of local plants and animals.

The longer mountain trail, though well-trodden, does not have toilet facilities or any places to rest and some parts can get slippery in rainy weather.

Please also note that there are no handrails along the track, so you are walking at your own risk.

The villagers are keen conservationists and work closely with environmental NGOs.

As a community run eco-tourism activity, a portion of your hiking fee also helps support education, healthcare, and sustainable development in Abaca Village.

item.title: MOTORING & ACCESSORIES | Luxury ride with so many new features
item.description: THE New Mazda CX-9 is all about design, comfort, luxury and technology which conveys a sense of strength and energy manifested in the New Mazda CX-9’s athletic stance, elongated hood and tapered roofline. The overall effect is a combination of composed powerful SUV with so many features.
item.content:encoded:
THE New Mazda CX-9 is all about design, comfort, luxury and technology which conveys a sense of strength and energy manifested in the New Mazda CX-9’s athletic stance, elongated hood and tapered roofline.

The overall effect is a combination of composed powerful SUV with so many features.

More confidence for more driving enjoyment.

Available predictive i-Activ AWD all-wheel drive monitors everything from outside temperature to throttle input and braking patterns.

Using that information, it adjusts power delivery to help give you optimal traction before your wheels can slip.

Even when the weather’s perfect, I-Activ AWD helps stabilize and improve the Mazda CX-9’s overall road grip by sensing changes in steering effort and traction.

Mazda Connect, Mazda’s infotainment system with Android Auto and Apple Car Play means you can connect your phone seamlessly so you can access your apps, work on the go and more via display screen, also contains USB ports.

Other notable features of the Mazda CX-9 include, Lane-keep assist system, Rear & front parking sensors, Mazda Radar Cruise Control with Stop & Go.

Mazda Advanced Keyless Entry and Start System, Blind spot monitoring with rear cross traffic alert, Rain sensoring wipers, LED headlights with auto on/off and auto height control and LED combination taillights, Premium leather seats, Power sliding moon roof with one-touch open feature & interior sunshade, and Bose Centerpoint 2 Surround Sound System with Audio Pilot 2 and 6 speakers.

A Difference you can feel in performance, design, technology, style and control with 5 star i-Activ-sense safety technology helps alert you of hazards to avoid collisions.

Another way the Mazda CX-9 elevates the driving experience is by subduing road, wind and engine noise, used for sound-insulating glass, and installed even better seal insulation than in the previous generation.

All this makes the Mazda CX-9 remarkably quiet, ensuring your time behind the wheel is one of minimal stress and maximum enjoyment.

Mazda CX-9’s are available at Niranjans Mazda Dealership in Suva, Nadi, Lautoka and Labasa.

Interior with features of Mazda CX-9. Pictures: Niranjans

Right: Rear side view of New Mazda CX-9.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: MOTORING & ACCESSORIES | 2026 SUBARU CROSSTREK 2.0L SUV
item.description: The new 2026 Subaru Crosstrek has been a trusted partner for customers interested in drivability, utility, safety and those who enjoy nature and are involved in outdoor sports and activities. Subaru Crosstrek features have been created to cater for the present and future needs of customers from design, craftsmanship, quality, to engineering and technology, the New Subaru SUV is truly the best Cros
item.content:encoded:
The new 2026 Subaru Crosstrek has been a trusted partner for customers interested in drivability, utility, safety and those who enjoy nature and are involved in outdoor sports and activities.

Subaru Crosstrek features have been created to cater for the present and future needs of customers from design, craftsmanship, quality, to engineering and technology, the New Subaru SUV is truly the best Crosstrek has ever built.

The New Subaru Crosstrek is a subcompact crossover which offers style, versatility, specs, robustness, rugged, fuel efficiency, engineering & most of all – Affordability.

In short, the New 2026 Crosstrek is a package designed to go slightly farther off the pavement than its previous model – The Subaru XV.

The Subaru Crosstrek is available in 2 models: Base Spec & Mid Spec which is equipped with the Subaru Eyesight Driver Assist Technology.

Both, the base Spec and the mid Spec get a 2.0-liter four cylinder Horizontally Opposed (Boxer) – Euro 6 Engine which punches up to 115kw of power.

This engine is the carryover from its Ex-model – providing an excellent fuel economy of 9.7Litre per 100km on Urban and 5.9Litre per 100km on Highway.

Also carrying over from the previous model is the CVT – Continuous Variable Transmission – providing the Crosstrek the ability to deliver the right gear change for optimum performance for daily driving.

The Crosstrek has an improved AWD system which offers quicker response to changing driving conditions and road surface.

Hill start assist is standard, along with Subaru’s X-Mode traction and drive mode technology, which includes hill descent control.

Subaru offers two different approaches to the Crosstrek’s infotainment system.

The base model features a split dual 7-inch touchscreen displays while the Mid Spec, boasts a sharp-looking and responsive 11.6-inch touchscreen display mounted in portrait orientation that dominates the center of the dashboard. The 2026 Crosstrek in mid spec has been upgraded with extra features such as: day time running lights, Led Fog Lamps, a full 11.6″ touch screen and the Subaru Eyesight Driver Assist Technology which offers – Pre-Collision Braking System, Adaptive Cruise Control, Lane Departure Warning, Lead Vehicle Star Alert and Reverse Sensors with Reverse Automatic Braking The main focus for the 2026 Crosstrek is to “bring joy and smiles to our adventure-seeking customers” for every day of the week including vacations.

That means comfort not just in the daily gridlock but on long road trips as well.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: MOTORING & ACCESSORIES | The story behind The Queen of the Jungle
item.description: While most people are still asleep, Aisea Burelevu Naqa is already on the road. At 4am, beneath the darkness of the Naitasiri highlands, he starts the engine of the “Queen of the Jungle” a Toyota Hilux that has become a lifeline for the people of Rewasau in the tikina of Nabaubuco Naitasiri. As the vehicle makes its way along the rugged roads towards Suva, carrying farmers, market vendors and thei
item.content:encoded:
While most people are still asleep, Aisea Burelevu Naqa is already on the road. At 4am, beneath the darkness of the Naitasiri highlands, he starts the engine of the “Queen of the Jungle” a Toyota Hilux that has become a lifeline for the people of Rewasau in the tikina of Nabaubuco Naitasiri.

As the vehicle makes its way along the rugged roads towards Suva, carrying farmers, market vendors and their produce, Aisea’s mission is simple, to serve his community.

For the past year, the 4 -seater carrier has been the primary means of transport for villagers after bus services ceased operating in the area.

Bought outright by the mataqali, the vehicle was intended to help villagers transport goods to the market while generating income for community development projects. “The vehicle was bought by the mataqali to help our people,” Aisea said.

“It allows farmers to take their produce to Suva and earn an income while also helping us raise funds for village projects.”

The initiative was expected to contribute towards improvements such as footpaths and other community developments. However, rising fuel prices have made achieving those goals increasingly difficult.

“The biggest challenge we are facing is the cost of fuel.

“A return trip can cost around $200 in fuel alone. At the moment, almost everything we earn goes straight back into keeping the vehicle on the road.”

Passengers currently pay $25 for a trip to the capital, a price Aisea has deliberately kept unchanged despite increasing operating costs.

“I know the struggles our people face every day,”

“Most of them are farmers who work hard to provide for their families. I cannot increase the fare because they are already trying to make ends meet.”

It is a sacrifice that leaves little room for profit, but one he is willing to make for the benefit of the community.

For Aisea, the role is much more than driving. It is about responsibility, service and trust.

“I am grateful to the mataqali for entrusting me with this vehicle.

“It is a responsibility I take seriously because so many people depend on it.”

Driving has been part of his life for more than 12 years.

Before taking charge of the Queen of the Jungle, he spent years behind the wheel of a Viti Minibus operating along the busy Suva-Lautoka corridor.

Today, his route may be different, but his commitment remains the same.

Away from work, Aisea is a devoted husband and father. His wife is from Tailevu and together they have raised four beautiful children.

Two are now employed while the younger two are pursuing tertiary studies at the Fiji National University.

His faith continues to guide him through the challenges of life.

“God has a plan for everything, I always remember John 1:3: ‘Through Him all things were made, without Him nothing was made that has been made. That verse gives me strength every day.”

As dawn breaks and another journey begins, the Queen of the Jungle continues its daily trek between Rewasau and Suva.

At the wheel is a man driven not by profit, but by purpose.

For Aisea Burelevu Naqa, every kilometre travelled is another opportunity to serve his people, strengthen his community and keep Rewasau moving forward.

Aisea’s mission is simple, to serve his Mataqali and his people. Picture: MELI NAGUSUDRAU

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: PROPERTY GUIDE | Easy-to-clean homes
item.description: A more practical and functional home is a home that makes life easier for its occupants, day after day. No-one wants to live in spaces that are unenjoyable, badly exploited, difficult to clean and manage. And one should not think that practicality and functionality are in contrast with beauty, on the contrary: today, technological innovation allows architecture and design to propose solutions that
item.content:encoded:
A more practical and functional home is a home that makes life easier for its occupants, day after day. No-one wants to live in spaces that are unenjoyable, badly exploited, difficult to clean and manage.

And one should not think that practicality and functionality are in contrast with beauty, on the contrary: today, technological innovation allows architecture and design to propose solutions that are both practical and aesthetically beautiful.

Besides order, there is another word that comes closest to the ideal of a practical and functional home: cleanliness.

In fact, tidiness and cleanliness are two concepts that often go hand in hand: if you have one but not the other, your home looks neglected and decidedly impractical.

Cleaning the house is almost never a pleasant activity. Some things need to be cleaned every day, while others need to be cleaned less frequently, but very thoroughly.

Cleaning the house can often cause tiredness, dissatisfaction and a feeling of wasted time.

To avoid this, and to achieve a much more practical and functional home, you can make life easier by opting as much as possible for objects, spaces and materials that are easy to clean.

An example of this is offered by aluminium, a material that can be chosen for various furnishing solutions and, above all, in the case of fixtures.

In addition to its high performance, aluminium is an extremely easy material to clean: it does not stain easily, it does not absorb grease or oil, it does not retain dust, and unsightly halos are unlikely to form on it.

It is therefore the best ally one can choose, as it only needs to be cleaned occasionally with a damp cloth and a neutral detergent.

A minimal style is definitely the best choice for a practical and functional, as well as aesthetically beautiful, home.

Minimalism means having few objects, but of great quality. Following the concept of less is more, cleaning also becomes much easier.

This concept can then be combined with elements that make cleaning much easier; for example, sofas with removable covers, curtains that can be easily removed for washing and, in general, fabrics and colours that are difficult to stain and that are easy to wash.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | VERE’S TAKE | What’s in the budget for you?
item.description: Bula Vinaka shoppers, The hot topic in many households today is the rising cost of living. From the price of fuel to the cost of basic groceries, we are all feeling the pressure as we struggle to make ends meet at home. The other day, I went to fill up my car and realised just how much more I had to pay. What used to cost me $40 to top-up ended up costing $75. It was a stark reminder of how rising
item.content:encoded:
Bula Vinaka shoppers, The hot topic in many households today is the rising cost of living. From the price of fuel to the cost of basic groceries, we are all feeling the pressure as we struggle to make ends meet at home.

The other day, I went to fill up my car and realised just how much more I had to pay. What used to cost me $40 to top-up ended up costing $75. It was a stark reminder of how rising fuel prices is affecting everyone. A trip to the market in downtown Suva tells a similar story.

A tray of eggs now costs up to $20, and the prices of many essential items keeps creeping up week after week. Last Friday, I noticed something unusual at a supermarket that is normally packed around 7pm.

A cashier told me customers were more cautious with their spending and only buying what they needed.

Arun from Dilkusha raised concerns over the high cost of imported fruits and vegetables, pointing to avocados at $36 per kilogram, capsicums at $24.99/kg, raspberries at $127.60/kg, mandarins at $25/kg, tomatoes at $29/kg, broccoli at $31/kg and celery at $8.99/kg. She said similar produce was cheaper in Australia and New Zealand, citing celery at 79 cents a head and zucchini at 69 cents a kilogram. She believes supermarkets need to negotiate better deals with suppliers and lower their profit margins to make these products more affordable. Red onions and red cabbages have also become unaffordable.

Most families are cutting back, changing their shopping habits and finding new ways to cope with increases. Against this backdrop, all eyes are on the 2026–2027 National Budget, delivered in Parliament today.

There is hope that it will provide some relief at a time when household finances are under significant strain.

The Government has indicated the budget will focus on easing the cost of living, support businesses and economic stability, maintain critical public services and ensure fiscal discipline while introducing reforms for growth. The budget, we hope will bring relief to the low to mid income earners.

This as we continues to face economic pressures from rising fuel prices, increasing public debt, inflation concerns and uncertainty in the global economy. Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka says concerns raised during the recent State of the Fijian Economy Dialogue were considered as the Government finalised the budget, although details remain confidential until the official announcement today.

For ordinary citizens like Kata, the biggest hope is simple that the budget will bring lower prices for goods and services to allow him to spend less when buying food and paying for services.

However, he was quick to acknowledge that immediate reductions may not be realistic given the wider economic challenges.

Vili shares a more sceptical view, saying it is difficult to expect positive change when leaders do not feel the hardships faced by the grassroots people as they earn high salaries.

He said parliamentarians were paid by poor taxpayers funds and drove in posh vehicles without paying anything.

These opinions reflect the expectations and frustrations of many families.

However, it’s still important to understand that a national budget is much more than a list of taxes, spending plans and government announcements.

It is one of the Government’s most important tools for managing the economy and responding to the needs of its people. It certainly determines how much money is collected and where it will be spent.

The choices made influence almost every aspect of daily life, from healthcare and education to roads, infrastructure, social welfare, agriculture, job creation and support for businesses. A good budget must strike a careful balance.

It needs to provide relief for families struggling today while also managing government debt and investing in Fiji’s long-term development.

This balance is especially important for Fiji as a small island nation vulnerable to global fuel and food price shocks, climate-related disasters and changes in key sectors such as tourism.

The budget must therefore not only address the challenges of today but also prepare the country for the uncertainties of tomorrow.

As Fijians listen to today’s budget announcement, the question on many peole’s minds is simple: Will it make life a little easier for ordinary families feeling the pinch of the war and the rising costs of fuel and food?

Everyone wants to spend less to make life more manageable in these challenging economic times.

Let’s wait and see what our Government has to offer us!

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | The floor beneath your feet | The quiet power of good tiles at Vinod Patel
item.description: There is a building material so common we walk over it every day without a second thought, yet so important that its absence is felt immediately. Tiles have been used to define the character of a space for thousands of years from the mosaic floors of ancient Rome to the clean porcelain bathrooms of modern Fiji. They are functional, beautiful, and when chosen well, they are the single detail that t
item.content:encoded:
There is a building material so common we walk over it every day without a second thought, yet so important that its absence is felt immediately. Tiles have been used to define the character of a space for thousands of years from the mosaic floors of ancient Rome to the clean porcelain bathrooms of modern Fiji. They are functional, beautiful, and when chosen well, they are the single detail that ties an entire room together.

A tile does more than cover a surface. It manages moisture, repels bacteria, withstands heat, and survives decades of foot traffic without complaint.

In Fiji’s tropical climate, where humidity is high and the sun beats hard, the right floor tile keeps surfaces cool underfoot and prevents the moisture damage that destroys timber and carpet over time. A ceramic or porcelain tile laid correctly is arguably the most durable flooring choice available to a Fijian homeowner.

The right tile transforms a house into a home — and lasts a lifetime.

Wall tiles go further still. In bathrooms and kitchens, they create a fully waterproof surface that can be wiped clean in seconds eliminating the mould problems that plague painted walls in wet areas. In living spaces and outdoor entertainment areas, a well-chosen wall tile adds texture, depth, and architectural character that paint simply cannot achieve. The Nanhai Porcelain Matt Rustic range, for instance, brings the raw texture of natural stone to any wall at a fraction of the cost of quarried material.

The shift to 600×600 and larger format tiles in recent years has transformed interior design across the Pacific. Large format tiles create the illusion of more space fewer grout lines mean the eye travels further before stopping, making even a modest room feel expansive and modern.

The Lezora 600×600 Porcelain Gloss range achieves this effect beautifully, its reflective surface bouncing light around the room and amplifying natural brightness.

From the entry porch to the master bathroom, from outdoor patios to commercial shop floors, tiles are the foundation on which the character of a building is built. Vinod Patel’s Mega Tiles Sale running from 1st June to 19th July 2026 brings Fiji’s widest range of floor and wall tiles to you at up to 50% off regular price. Whether you are building new, renovating, or simply refreshing a tired room, there has never been a better time to tile.

For many Fijian families, the gap between dreaming of a beautifully tiled home and actually having one comes down to a single word: cash. Large tiling projects require purchasing by the carton, and the upfront cost of tiling a bathroom, kitchen, and living area can easily run into several thousand dollars.

That financial hurdle has historically pushed renovation and building projects back by months or years projects started when money is available rather than when they should be done.

Vinod Patel’s Hire Purchase programme changes that equation entirely. Through a simple online application, customers can purchase tiles and any other home and building product and spread the full cost over 36 months with zero deposit required. There are no large upfront payments, no need to drain savings, and no delays waiting for funds to accumulate.

You choose your tiles today, they go home with you today, and you pay in comfortable monthly instalments that fit within a regular household budget.

$0 deposit. 36 months. Fast approvals. Your tiles go home today.

The application process is fast and straightforward. Customers apply online through vinodpatel.com.fj, Fiji’s only dedicated home and building online store, and receive a response quickly often the same day. Approval requires minimal documentation, and once approved, the full purchase value is available immediately. There is no need to visit a bank or complete complex paperwork. The entire process is designed to remove friction and get your project moving.

Shop Anywhere, Any Time www.vinodpatel.com.fj

Fiji’s homes are being built and renovated from every corner of the archipelago. A builder in Labasa, a homeowner in Savusavu, a contractor finishing a resort fit out in Taveuni all of them need the same access to quality building materials that has historically been available only to those close to a major branch. Vinod Patel’s online store at vinodpatel.com.fj changes that permanently.

Launched as Fiji’s only dedicated home and building online store, vinodpatel.com.fj brings the full Vinod Patel product range — including the entire Mega Tiles Sale catalogue to any device with an internet connection. Browse tiles by category, size, finish, or price.

Check availability. And place your order without leaving your home, office, or worksite.

Critically, the Hire Purchase application is available directly through the website. Customers browsing tiles online can proceed from product selection to HP application in the same session without visiting a branch, without printing forms, and without waiting.

The digital HP process is designed to mirror the speed and simplicity of the in-store experience, bringing the same fast-approval finance to customers wherever they are in Fiji.

The Mega tiles sale is on now. up to 50% off. shop in store or online at vinodpatel.com.fj. apply for 36-month Hire Purchase with $0 deposit. while stocks last.

Tiles are available in a wide range of colors.

Stylish and durable bathroom tiles for every home.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Made of ingredients that transform the skin
item.description: VASELINE Gluta Hya 70x is one of most advanced skincare offered by Vaseline. Designed to deliver powerful brightening and hydration benefits, it is part of the Healthy Bright Gluta Hya Serum Burst range and is formulated to tackle dullness, uneven skin tone, and dryness. A standout feature is its GlutaGlow™ technology, which is 70x times more powerful than Vitamin C in fighting dullness and restor
item.content:encoded:
VASELINE Gluta Hya 70x is one of most advanced skincare offered by Vaseline. Designed to deliver powerful brightening and hydration benefits, it is part of the Healthy Bright Gluta Hya Serum Burst range and is formulated to tackle dullness, uneven skin tone, and dryness. A standout feature is its GlutaGlow™ technology, which is 70x times more powerful than Vitamin C in fighting dullness and restoring radiance. This makes it a compelling choice for individuals seeking visible improvements in skin clarity and brightness.

Vasleine Gluta Hya combines several key ingredients that work together to transform the skin. GlutaGlow™ technology targets dullness and its root, helping to reduce dark spots and improve overall skin tone.

Alongside this, the 4D Hyaluron complex provides multi-layer hydration by using hyaluron molecules of different sizes, ensuring that moisture penetrates deeply and keeps the skin supple throughout the day.

Another feature of Vaseline Gluta Hya 70x is its sun protection. With SPF50 Plus luminous protection, it shields your skin from harmful sun rays, preventing sun damage and premature aging. The lightweight serum burst formula absorbs quickly, leaving no greasy residue, which makes it convenient for everyday application.

The versatility of this product makes it suitable for various occasions. It can be used as daily sun protection, as post-sun care to restore hydration, or as part of an evening routine to nourish and repair your skin overnight. Its lightweight texture ensures that it fits seamlessly into any skincare regimen without feeling heavy or sticky.

Using Vaseline Gluta Hya 70x is simple. It is recommended to apply twice daily, massaging thoroughly onto the body or face to ensure even coverage. Consistency is key, as results are visible with regular use rather overnight.

It can also be used for both morning and evening routines, while also as a sun protection for the Luminous Defense range.

A product of Unilever, Vaseline is distributed in Fiji by Motibhai Group, sharing quality, sharing happiness.

Visit the Vaseline booth at the 2026 Fiji Showcase for exclusive deals.

Vasleine Gluta Hya combines several key ingredients that work together to transform the skin.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Keeping your kitchen clean matters more than you think
item.description: TIMES SHOPPER | Keeping your kitchen clean matters more than you think
